text.title: OpenAI Function Calling Loop and Parallel Calls
text.summary: How to define tools, run the request-execute-respond loop, and handle parallel tool calls safely with the OpenAI SDK.

rule.statement: Append the assistant message (with tool_calls) to messages before appending tool results; sort tool results by tool_call_id before the next API call.
rule.rationale: The OpenAI API requires the assistant message with tool_calls to appear before any tool result messages. Result order matters for the model's next reasoning step.

example.code:
import json from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
messages = [{"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather in Kyiv?"}] response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=messages, tools=tools)
message = response.choices[0].message messages.append(message) # must append before tool results
if message.tool_calls: for tc in message.tool_calls: args = json.loads(tc.function.arguments) result = tool_registry[tc.function.name](**args) messages.append({ "role": "tool", "tool_call_id": tc.id, "content": json.dumps(result), }) final = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=messages, tools=tools) print(final.choices[0].message.content)

rule.statement: Validate tool call coherence before executing parallel calls — the model may batch contradictory calls (create and delete the same resource).
rule.rationale: OpenAI enables parallel tool calls by default. Without coherence checking, contradictory parallel calls can corrupt state before either result is returned to the model.

rule.statement: Use tool_choice="required" to force at least one tool call; use {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "..."}} to force a specific tool.
rule.rationale: tool_choice="auto" (default) lets the model skip tool use entirely when it believes an in-context answer is sufficient. Use "required" when the pipeline depends on a tool result.
